title: Forward inner call revert reason to caller using returndatacopy
description: When an external call reverts, the revert reason is available via returndatacopy. This pattern copies the full returndata into memory and re-reverts with it, preserving the original error selector and parameters. Without this, the caller would only see a generic revert without context.
when_to_apply: Any external call where the inner revert reason should be visible to the end user
when_not_to_apply: When you want to replace the inner revert with a custom error regardless of what the callee returned
